requirements: The portal must allow patients to book appointments based on doctor availability.
Patients shall be able to view their medical history and test results.
Prescription refill requests must be sent directly to the pharmacy.
The system must notify patients for upcoming health screenings.
Access to the portal must require multi-factor authentication for PHI.
All medical records shall be stored in a FHIR-compliant format.
Waitlist alerts shall notify patients of earlier slot availability.
The dashboard must display a summary of current medications and dosages.
Patients shall be able to message their providers securely via the app.
Billing statements must include clear itemized insurance coverage details.
The system shall support proxy access for legal guardians and caregivers.
Vitals data from wearables must be synced into the patient record.
nfrs: Patient data privacy must strictly follow HIPAA/HITECH regulations.
All PHI must be encrypted using AES-256 for data at rest.
The portal shall load patient records in under 3 seconds over 4G.
Audit logs must record every access to sensitive health information.

Provider dashboard for clinical task management

Compliance & FDA Regulatory.text: FDA Class I medical software registration
Audit trail for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance

Stakeholders (Care Teams/IT).text: Chief Medical Information Officer as sponsor
IT Security team for HIPAA validation

requirements: The platform must support high-definition video consultations.
E-prescriptions must be generated and sent electronically to pharmacies.
Patient consent forms must be digitally signed before every visit.
Insurance eligibility verification shall be automated within the app.
Waiting room dashboard shall update in real-time for providers.
Follow-up scheduling must occur immediately after the session ends.
The platform shall allow screen sharing for imaging and reports.
Providers must be able to document clinical notes during the call.
Translation services integration shall be available for multi-lingual care.
Billing events must be triggered automatically upon consultation close.
Patients shall be able to upload photos of symptoms for review.
Network quality monitoring must alert users of poor connections.
nfrs: Video call latency must stay under 150ms for smooth clinical interaction.
Video and audio streams must be end-to-end encrypted (HIPAA).
The system must maintain 99.95% availability for urgent care calls.
Platform must be compatible with Chrome, Safari, and dedicated mobile apps.

Patient Safety & Risk Mitigation.text: Emergency protocol trigger for critical vitals
Session recording policy for clinical audit

Automated clinical note drafting via AI

Data residency in national medical clouds

requirements: The system shall display real-time vitals for all ICU patients in one view.
Audible alerts must trigger for critical heart rate and O2 fluctuations.
Nurse workflow dashboard must prioritize task lists by patient severity.
Device integration shall support monitors, ventilators, and infusion pumps.
The system must maintain a full audit trail of all clinical actions.
Shift handover reports shall be generated automatically from the system.
Trend analysis must highlight deteriorating patient conditions over 24h.
The interface must support touch-screen interaction for sterile environments.
Early Warning Scores (EWS) must be calculated and shown prominently.
Medication administration records (MAR) must sync with the pharmacy.
System shall support remote monitoring for consulting specialists.
Emergency response protocols shall be displayed on the screen during codes.
nfrs: System failure must trigger an immediate failsafe hard-wired alarm.
Vitals data processing must maintain sub-100ms end-to-end latency.
Interface must remain readable under various hospital lighting conditions.
Local server redundancy is required for 100% vital data continuity.

Central station monitoring for ICU nurses

requirements: The system shall manage drug inventory with real-time stock levels.
Dispensing module must verify the drug against the e-prescription.
Automatic drug interaction checks must run for every newly filled script.
The system must facilitate DEA-compliant tracking for controlled substances.
Insurance claim adjudication shall happen instantly at the point of sale.
Pharmacies must be able to manage multiple locations from one dashboard.
Automatic refill reminders shall be sent to patients via SMS.
Barcode scanning must be used at every step of the filling process.
The system shall generate daily narcotics disposal reports.
Integration with state PDMP databases for controlled substance history.
Patient education sheets must be printed automatically with every drug.
Compound pharmacy capabilities must support manual formula entry.
nfrs: All controlled substance data must have tamper-proof audit trails.
The system must handle 10,000 prescriptions per day per location.
Point of sale transactions must complete in less than 5 seconds.
Offline mode must allow critical dispensing if the internet fails.

Data Privacy (PHI / HIPAA / GDPR).text: PHI masking on public-facing pharmacy screens
Pharmacist-only access to patient health history

requirements: The system must track patient enrollment and screening against criteria.
Clinical protocol adherence shall be monitored via electronic checklists.
Adverse event reporting must be immediate and follow regulatory paths.
The platform must support Electronic Data Capture (EDC) from study sites.
Statistical analysis plans must be integrated for automated reports.
The system shall follow FDA 21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records.
Randomization and trial supply management (RTSM) must be automated.
Monitors shall have a dashboard for site progress and data queries.
Patient-reported outcomes (ePRO) shall be captured via mobile app.
The platform must support multi-center and global trial coordination.
Audit trail must include reason for any clinical data change.
System shall facilitate regulatory submission and eDMS integration.
nfrs: Clinical data must be immutable once the trial is locked.
21 CFR Part 11 compliance for electronic signatures is mandatory.
System must maintain high availability for critical safety monitoring.
Global compliance with ICH-GCP standards is required everywhere.
